page: Microsoft Azure EC4es_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEV5 server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and an Intel processor. The system is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server type does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached options, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance. Additionally, the EC4es_v5 does not feature any graphics processing units, with a GPU count of zero.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density relative to the processing core count position this instance as a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff that requires external storage configuration. This virtual machine is designed for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics tasks that require a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring local temporary disks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC4es_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEV5 family, powered by an Intel processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance does not include any local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, requiring external network-attached storage configurations. It contains zero GPUs and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density make this server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is suited for applications such as in-memory caching, relational databases, and data analytics that demand significant memory capacity relative to compute resources without the need for local temporary storage.
